name: Security
description: Cryptography, penetration testing, threat modeling, and defensive security
axioms: Kerckhoffs's principle: security should depend on key secrecy, not algorithm secrecy
Defense in depth: multiple layers of security, no single point of failure
Least privilege: grant minimum permissions required for task
Fail secure: default to deny access on error, not allow
Security is a process, not a product (Schneier)
Attacker needs one vulnerability; defender must protect everything
reasoning_patterns: Threat modeling: identify assets, threats, vulnerabilities, and mitigations
Attack surface analysis: enumerate all entry points and trust boundaries
Cryptographic protocol analysis: verify key exchange, authentication, confidentiality
Code review for security: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, buffer overflow patterns
Incident response: detect, contain, eradicate, recover, lessons learned
boundary_conditions: Does not cover physical security in depth
Does not cover social engineering in detail
Formal verification of security properties is specialized
key_references: Stallings, W. Cryptography and Network Security
canonical_truths: Never roll your own crypto (use vetted libraries)
MD5 and SHA-1 are broken for collision resistance
TLS 1.3 is the current standard for transport encryption
SQL injection is still the #1 web vulnerability after 20+ years
Zero-trust architecture: never trust, always verify (even internal traffic)
